verdict: Lead costs sit near industry average, but pipeline value is zero — revenue tracking is the gap to fix.
diagnosis: This period delivered 30 leads at $158 each from $4,733 in Instagram spend, but zero dollars are attached to any opportunity in your CRM.

signals.headline: 30 leads at $158 each
signals.detail: Cost per lead landed near the home services average for Facebook and Instagram ads this period. Volume is steady, not exceptional.

signals.headline: Zero revenue tracked on 30 leads
signals.detail: Every opportunity shows $0 value and 'open' status. You cannot calculate return on ad spend without job values entered.

signals.headline: 49 calls, 82 direction requests
signals.detail: Google Business Profile drove strong local intent this period. Direction requests outpaced calls, suggesting walk-in or scheduled visits.

signals.headline: 858 reviews at 5.0 stars
signals.detail: Reputation is a major asset. This is well above what most local competitors carry and should be leveraged in ads.

signals.headline: Latest day shows zero spend
signals.detail: Most recent day reports no impressions or spend. Check whether the campaign paused, hit budget, or has a reporting delay.

recommendations.title: Fix revenue tracking in your CRM before scaling spend
recommendations.detail: Every opportunity shows $0. Consider requiring a job value field on every lead so you can see which ads produce real revenue, not just form fills.

recommendations.title: Test Google Local Services Ads alongside Instagram
recommendations.detail: Worth exploring given your 5-star rating across 858 reviews. Lead costs on that channel typically run $25 to $80 for HVAC, well below your current $158.

recommendations.title: Audit speed-to-lead on the 30 Meta form fills
recommendations.detail: Consider timing how fast someone calls each lead back. Responding within a minute can lift conversion meaningfully, especially for AC inquiries in peak summer.
